title: Synchronous process spawning inside sort comparator during forensic entry-point ranking
description: The forensic scanner sorts all detected entry points with a comparator calling readGitChurnWeight per comparison, which synchronously spawns a child process (execFileSync git log) with no caching. O(N log N) comparisons cause hundreds of synchronous spawns (50 files -> 500+ spawns), freezing the CLI, starving the event loop, and risking FD exhaustion.
fix_direction: Pre-compute git churn weight per entry point once (or a lazy cache Map), then do fast in-memory numeric comparison during sort.

title: O(N^2) array linear scanning in recall ID path-filtering & intersection
description: Combined recall filters effective IDs against candidate IDs via effectiveIds.filter(id => candidateIds.includes(id)), then dedupes preserving order with a loop calling requestedIds.includes(id). Both arrays can grow to the retrieval limit N (or larger if a client passes a huge ID array), so .includes() inside .filter()/loop is O(N^2), costly at hundreds of IDs.
fix_direction: Convert candidateIds and requestedIds to Sets for O(1) .has() lookups, reducing to O(N).

title: Unbounded HTTP session growth (no cleanup / idle expiration)
description: The experimental HTTP server holds all active SSE sessions in a module-level Map. onsessionclosed only deletes on clean SSE teardown; half-open connections, network drops, or repeated init requests accumulate sessions indefinitely. With no reaping/TTL/idle timeout the session map grows unbounded, a slow memory leak and eventual OOM under high concurrent traffic.
fix_direction: Add session eviction: a background reaper for sessions idle > TTL (e.g. 5 min), or cap max concurrent sessions.

title: Hardcoded candidate/sample limits silently truncate forensic analysis
description: The forensic scanner hardcodes SAMPLE_LIMIT=5 and CANDIDATE_FILE_LIMIT=12, silently truncating analysis. In large projects with many entry points or multi-framework topologies, only the first 5 entry points are analyzed and at most 12 candidate files output, causing incomplete framework recommendations and missed files at scale.
fix_direction: Move these limits to user-configurable / project config (fabric-config.json) or scale dynamically with project topology size.

title: Silent SSE ring-buffer truncation causes replay out-of-sync
description: The SSE events handler uses a fixed RING_BUFFER_CAPACITY=50 for replay history. If a client misses more than 50 events (disconnect or a burst like bulk save/re-index), older events are silently evicted; on reconnect with lastEventId the buffer cannot replay the oldest missed events, leaving the client out-of-sync with no warning.
fix_direction: Make ring-buffer capacity configurable/dynamic, or detect a lastEventId already evicted and return an explicit out_of_sync status prompting a full reload.
